channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Apple denies wrongdoing but settles to pay $95 million cash (about $20 per class member) in Siri privacy suit
item.description: Apple agreed to pay $95 million in cash to settle a proposed class action lawsuit claiming that its voice-activated Siri assistant violated users’ privacy. A preliminary settlement was filed on Tuesday night in the Oakland, California federal court, and requires approval by U.S.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: Apple to pay $95 million to settle lawsuit accusing Siri of snoopy eavesdropping
item.description: Apple has agreed to pay $95 million to settle a lawsuit accusing the privacy-minded company of deploying its virtual assistant Siri to eavesdrop on people using its iPhone and other trendy devices.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: US CPSC: Apple's AirTag Now Compliant with Reese's Law - Consumer Safety Updates
item.description: Apple's AirTag now features additional warnings about potential battery hazards, aligning with Reese's Law in the US. Learn more about consumer safety updates and product compliance.
item.dc:creator: jacob.krol@futurenet.com (Jacob Krol), TechRadar

item.title: The Ultimate Guide to Online Sleuthing: Tips and Tricks
item.description: Learn how to verify a website, find out what's online about you, and even uncover scammers with these simple tech tricks.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: California's Winter Snowpack Off to Solid Start in 2025
item.description: California begins 2025 with solid start to winter snowpack, but officials caution more storms are needed to keep water supplies on track.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Indiana Bill Seeks to Block Kids from Social Media Without Parental Consent
item.description: A new bill in Indiana aims to protect kids from social media's potential dangers, but experts say it may not be the best solution.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Adamson, NewsNation

item.title: Americans more likely to embrace driverless cars, but still skeptical: Survey
item.description: Americans are more likely to embrace driverless cars but are still skeptical of them, according to a new survey from YouGov. In the survey, 35 percent of Americans said when it comes to driverless cars’ advancement, they are “very enthusiastic” or “somewhat enthusiastic.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: GOP lawmakers request Yellen briefing on Chinese hackers' Treasury breach
item.description: Republican lawmakers are swiftly urging the Treasury Department to provide answers on a recent cybersecurity attack from China that infiltrated various workstations, as outlined in a letter obtained by The Hill. “This breach of federal government information is extremely concerning.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: How a flying ferry could help solve Tahoe’s traffic problem
item.description: "Hydrofoil" technology would transport passengers across Lake Tahoe in a half hour, potentially reducing traffic.
item.dc:creator: Lisa M. Krieger, The Mercury News

item.title: 4 keys for writing cross-platform apps
item.description: These days, most every application has the potential to be a cross-platform application. That’s great for users but often difficult for developers.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Ford’s X account calls Israel a ‘terrorist state’ in apparent hack incident
item.description: The official account for Ford Motor Co. on the social platform X was apparently compromised in a hacking incident behind a viral post calling Israel a “terrorist state.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: Rhode Islanders' data was leaked from a cyberattack on state health benefits website
item.description: PROVIDENCE, R.I. (AP) — Cybercriminals who hacked Rhode Island’s system for health and benefits programs have released files to a site on the dark web, a scenario the state has been preparing for, Gov. Daniel McKee said Monday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Hands-on with Adobe’s Acrobat AI Assistant: AI for your PDFs
item.description: We may be on the brink of 2025, but PDFs are still unavoidable in the professional world. No matter what industry you work in, you’re bound to whittle away precious moments wading through reports, white papers, and other dense documents in that clunky-feeling form.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Maintaining compliance in government agencies with rich text editors
item.description: Learn how rich text editors help government agencies ensure compliance with data security, accessibility, and document standardization. The post Maintaining compliance in government agencies with rich text editors first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: The second shoe is about to drop on a big DoD cybersecurity program
item.description: The DoD's big cybersecurity program advanced earlier this month. It's a big rule to carry out if it becomes effective. The post The second shoe is about to drop on a big DoD cybersecurity program first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Tom Temin, Federal News Network
